fact: October 7 is exactly 280 days into the year - that's the same number of days a human baby grows inside its mom!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts around this time, hiding up to 10,000 acorns each before winter hits!
The sun is setting almost 2 minutes earlier each day around October 7 in most places - darkness is gobbling up daylight super fast!
October 7 falls in the middle of Libra season, when the scales are perfectly balanced - just like how day and night were almost equal a few weeks ago!
In northern climates, maple trees are pumping out their brightest reds and oranges right now because they're basically bleeding sugar as they shut down for winter!
House spiders seem extra active around October 7 because this is typically when they're desperately trying to mate before winter!
The morning dew gets thicker around this date because the air can't hold as much water when it gets colder at night!
October 7 is roughly 85 days away from the winter solstice - we're sliding down the dark side of the year like a giant calendar slide!
In North America, monarch butterflies are flying over 2,000 miles south right now, often using the same trees their great-great-grandparents used as rest stops!
October 7 typically marks peak time when deciduous trees stop making the green chlorophyll that keeps them alive, revealing their hidden colors!
In northern regions, bats are eating extra right now - up to 1,000 mosquitoes per hour - to fatten up before their long winter sleep!
